title: DEALING WITH LOST LUGGAGE
conversation: Tom: Excuse me, I just arrived on the flight from Atlanta and my suitcase is missing. Jim: Did you wait until all the bags were unloaded? Tom: Yes, I did. My suitcase is not there. Jim: Here’s a card with various suitcases. Which looks most like your piece of luggage? Tom: It’s like this one, and it’s green. Jim: Okay, I’ll just have you fill out this paperwork. Tom: I had all my clothes for a meeting this afternoon in that bag. Now I’m in a bind. Jim: We’ll reimburse you for clothing you buy today for up to $100. Tom: I’m really pressed for time. I won’t have time to go shopping for a new suit now! Jim: Well, we’ll do our best to track down your bag as quickly as possible. Tom: What if my suitcase is lost for good? Jim: Baggage usually turns up, so let’s cross that bridge when we come to it. Tom: I’m really up the creek now. It boggles my mind how you can just lose someone’s luggage! Jim: Let me give you a piece of advice. Next time, wear your suit on the plane. Tom: Thanks for the advice. Next time I think I’ll fly a different airline!

idioms.meaning_en: to complete a form or document by providing information
idioms.meaning_vi: điền vào, hoàn tất (tờ khai, đơn từ)
idioms.example: Please fill out the application form and return it to the front desk.
idioms.context: Okay, I’ll just have you fill out this paperwork.

idioms.meaning_en: in a difficult or problematic situation
idioms.meaning_vi: trong tình thế khó khăn, kẹt vào thế bí
idioms.example: I'm in a bind because I have two meetings scheduled at the same time.

idioms.meaning_en: in a hurry; having very little time available
idioms.meaning_vi: gấp rút, không có nhiều thời gian
idioms.example: I'd love to stop and talk, but I'm really pressed for time right now.

idioms.meaning_en: to try as hard as possible to achieve something
idioms.meaning_vi: làm hết sức mình, cố gắng hết mức
idioms.example: We will do our best to ensure the event is a success.
idioms.context: Well, we’ll do our best to track down your bag as quickly as possible.

idioms.meaning_en: to find someone or something after a thorough search
idioms.meaning_vi: truy tìm, tìm ra dấu vết
idioms.example: It took me months to track down a rare copy of that book.
idioms.context: Well, we’ll do our best to track down your bag as quickly as possible.

idioms.example: After traveling for years, he decided to move back home for good.
idioms.context: What if my suitcase is lost for good?

idioms.meaning_en: to be found or appear, especially after being lost
idioms.meaning_vi: xuất hiện, được tìm thấy lại (sau khi thất lạc)
idioms.example: Don't worry about your keys; they usually turn up in the most obvious places.
idioms.context: Baggage usually turns up, so let’s cross that bridge when we come to it.

idioms.idiom: cross that bridge when we come to it
idioms.meaning_en: to deal with a problem only when it actually happens
idioms.meaning_vi: tới đâu hay tới đó, gặp chuyện rồi hãy lo
idioms.example: We don't know if the funding will be cut, so let's cross that bridge when we come to it.
idioms.context: Baggage usually turns up, so let’s cross that bridge when we come to it.

idioms.meaning_en: in a very difficult situation with no easy way out
idioms.meaning_vi: gặp rắc rối lớn, lâm vào cảnh khó khăn
idioms.example: If the bank doesn't approve our loan, we'll be up the creek.
idioms.context: I’m really up the creek now.

idioms.meaning_en: to be very surprising, confusing, or hard to imagine
idioms.meaning_vi: làm kinh ngạc, không thể tin nổi, không hiểu nổi
idioms.example: It boggles my mind how much technology has changed in just ten years.
idioms.context: It boggles my mind how you can just lose someone’s luggage!
